executive_summary.bottom_line: Most thesis-disconfirming week of trailing quarter. 30yr fell 16 bps to 6.30% but FOMC's hawkish hold drove 2026 cut probability from ~18% to ~3%, and Powell's exit hands the chair to Warsh, who has signaled he wants to abandon forward guidance and the dot plot entirely. Mechanical coiled spring unchanged at ~95% locked, but the policy path to 5.5% just lengthened materially.
executive_summary.coiled_spring_state: Mechanically loosened (rates -16 bps WoW, ~95.1% of mortgages still locked below current); nearest unlock at 6.25% would release ~5.4M owners and add ~1.5M SAAR — but with the path to 5.5% pushed out by the FOMC, this remains theoretical. No unlock-trigger threshold crossed.
executive_summary.whats_new: Two opposing shocks: (1) RE/MAX agreed to be acquired by Real Brokerage on April 27, RMAX +63% — basket-blind reading would see thesis confirmation; data says pure idiosyncratic M&A. (2) LGIH reported all-time-high 45.6% cancellation rate vs every other Tier-1 builder's cancel rate falling — entry-level cohort fracturing.

factor_scorecard.name: rate_lockin
factor_scorecard.signal: 30yr -16 bps WoW spot, but FOMC hold + Powell exit + 2026 cut odds 18→3% + Warsh anti-forward-guidance posture

factor_scorecard.signal: No new SFR REIT 10-Q this week; INVH/AMH last filings remain February 2026

factor_scorecard.signal: NAR pending sales -1.1% YoY despite spring; FHFA HPI Mountain -1.1% MoM; LGIH 45.6% cancel = entry-level cohort breaking; FHA delinquency 5.23% (Jan, +166 bps from Sep)

factor_scorecard.signal: FHFA HPI +1.7% YoY (slowest in cycle); no fresh CPI shelter print

factor_scorecard.name: cross_cutting
factor_scorecard.signal: FHFA/HUD VantageScore 4.0 + FICO 10T approval (Apr 22, +); FOMC hawkish hold (Apr 29, -); Iran war oil pass-through still elevated (-)

macro_deltas_material.comment: Forward supply weakening — divergence with starts notable

stock_signals.cause: Real Brokerage acquisition announced April 27; Zelman upgrade Underperform→Neutral $6.50 PT same day
stock_signals.thesis_read: Idiosyncratic M&A; no thesis read-across. The 1.5% Title Insurance basket median ex-RMAX is the better thesis tape — flat. RMAX is now an M&A spread name.
stock_signals.insider_flag: Adam Peterson (10%-owner) sold $11.6M across two trades on April 29; same-day execution into M&A spike at separate prices ($9.84, $10.97) suggests routed exit.

stock_signals.cause: 8-K April 30 with Items 1.01 (new agreement) + 1.02 (terminated agreement) + 2.03 (creation of direct financial obligation) = classic refinancing-under-duress pattern. Stock -8.7% April 29.
stock_signals.thesis_read: Tier-1 long-thesis name showing balance-sheet stress is dissonant with thesis. LDI mcap $523M is smallest of T1 originators (vs RKT $41B, UWMC $5.4B, PFSI $4.7B); not basket-fatal. Refi share LDI cannot capture goes to RKT/COOP/UWMC/PFSI.

stock_signals.cause: CEO Mat Ishbia sold $38M+ across 10 transactions in April (every 1-2 days; price range $3.71-$3.94). Likely 10b5-1 program — but cadence is heavy enough to suppress UWMC even when Factor 1 turns.
stock_signals.thesis_read: If rotating long-flow names within Mortgage Originators basket, RKT and PFSI carry less insider-supply overhang than UWMC.

stock_signals.cause: Q1 2026 cancellation rate 45.6% — all-time high in dataset. Up from 28.1% Q3 2025, 16.3% Q1 2025 (~3x YoY). Every other T1 builder's cancel rate is FALLING QoQ.
stock_signals.management_explanation: Cancels driven by buyers 'unable to qualify for financing'; LGIH deliberately extending pipeline times so customers can save for down payments / improve credit
stock_signals.thesis_read: Cleanest entry-level affordability-ceiling signal in the dataset. LGIH skews to FTHB/FHA cohort. Corroborated by Redfin's 13.4% market-wide March cancel rate and FHA delinquency rising from 3.57% (Sep) to 5.23% (Jan). Modestly thesis-disconfirming on Factor 4 ceiling: even if rates fall to 5.5%, FHA-leveraged cohort has taken income hits that the rate move alone may not heal.
stock_signals.counter_read: Backlog 1,699 units (+63% YoY, highest since Q1 2022). If half converts at 5.5% rates that's a real volume tailwind. Backlog quality is the question.

homebuilder_cohort_pattern.cancellation_rate_pattern: Every Tier-1 builder QoQ FALLING except LGIH (16.3 → 45.6) and CCS (n/a)
homebuilder_cohort_pattern.thesis_read: Diagonal gap between order intake and closing throughput is affordability ceiling biting at conversion stage. Builders' Q2-Q3 results entirely at mercy of where mortgage rates and FHA-cohort credit go. Move-up cohort (PHM, TMHC with ASP $542k/$578k) healthier than entry-level (LGIH, CCS).

subsector_basket_dispersion.read: Builders sold off despite strong order prints — FOMC overpowering operational beat
Moved against thesis; 'rates higher for longer = renters stay renters'
Also up over month, against thesis
Mostly flat; LDI drag offset by RKT/PFSI stability
Cohort discount-rate repricing on FOMC; BLDR -12.3%, SITE -12.7% standouts

correlation_thesis_validation.expected_behavior_this_week: Rate down 16 bps → these names should outperform
correlation_thesis_validation.actual_behavior: Mostly underperformed (homebuilder basket -5%); thesis-disconfirming. The forward-path / discount-rate channel dominated the spot-rate channel.

policy_events.event: FHFA + HUD authorize VantageScore 4.0 and FICO 10T
FOMC held 3.50-3.75% (vote 11-1, Miran dissent for cut); 2026 cut probability fell to ~3%
Warsh confirmation hearing — signals abandoning forward guidance and the dot plot, reverting to strict 2% inflation target
NAR Pending Home Sales March +1.5% MoM, -1.1% YoY
FHFA HPI February +1.7% YoY (slowest in cycle); Mountain -1.1% MoM, South Atlantic +0.6% MoM
Iran war oil pass-through: WTI peaked ~$94/bbl April; modeled >$80 throughout 2026 in 15% supply-shortfall base case
Eurozone CPI 3.0% in April from 2.6% in March on energy pass-through

key_risk.summary: Structural change in how rate expectations get priced, not the rate level itself. With Powell's last meeting on April 29 and Warsh succeeding in June: (a) realized cut path collapsed (CME 2026-cut probability ~3%), and (b) Warsh's stated intent to abandon forward guidance and the dot plot will likely WIDEN MBS-Treasury spread by removing a key channel for path compression. Mortgage spreads already 200-220 bps vs 160-180 bps historical. If oil sustains >$80 through Q2-Q3, every ingredient for the 30yr to retrace toward 5.5% moves the wrong direction.
key_risk.thesis_falsification: If this week's data is the new normal — operating names selling off on rate-down moves because the discount-rate channel dominates the spot-rate channel — then the thesis is right about the lockup but wrong about the unlock catalyst. Locked-low owners stay locked, but long-side beneficiaries don't re-rate when rates eventually fall, because the affordability-ceiling cohort has been hollowed out for so long that absorbed pent-up demand is smaller than thesis assumes.
key_risk.threshold: If <10% sustained through May, downgrade thesis time horizon by 6 months in next quarterly review. If oil >$80 through July, downgrade further.

underappreciated_catalyst.summary: FHFA/HUD VantageScore 4.0 + FICO 10T approval is being framed by mainstream coverage as a credit-scoring story. The second-order effect consensus is missing is on Factor 4 (demographics ceiling). Three pieces of evidence consensus is not connecting: (1) VantageScore 4.0 incorporates rent-payment history → expands qualifiable-borrower pool by ~4-7M consumers; (2) Pulte's 'stay tuned' on LLPAs = GSEs reserving option to price new-cohort loans differently; (3) Historical FICO 10T data publishes summer 2026 — if loss-rate distributions show parity, lenders adopt because LLPA pricing is accretive.
underappreciated_catalyst.consensus_missing: JCHS 2025 forecast of 8.6M household formation 2025-2035 does not appear to incorporate credit-eligibility expansion. If 30% of newly-qualifiable cohort converts to FTHB over 3-5 years, JCHS number too low by ~1-2M households.
underappreciated_catalyst.contrarian_read: While Factor 1 weakening for 6-12 months on FOMC + Warsh transition, Factor 4 structurally improving on credit-policy side — multi-year tailwind, not a quarter tailwind. Long-side names with high Factor 4 sensitivity (entry-level builders LGIH/CCS once cancel pulse stabilizes; FHA-cohort originators; manufactured housing/MH lots) are cheaper now than they would be if consensus correctly priced this.
underappreciated_catalyst.specific_catalyst: Summer 2026 FICO 10T historical-data publication. If GSE pricing grids (LLPAs) for VantageScore 4.0 print at parity or below equivalent Classic FICO, T1 originators with FHA exposure (LDI if it survives, RKT, UWMC) re-rate within a quarter on share-shift mechanics alone.

what_would_change_my_mind: April CPI shelter print (May 14) <0.25% MoM (consensus ~0.30%) — strengthens Factor 5 enough to offset this week's Factor 1 weakening. >0.40% extends Factor 5 stagnation; downgrade thesis-timing by another quarter.

MBA applications May 7 print >+5% after this week's -1.6% (April 22 was +7.9%). Two consecutive +5% prints would suggest the rate-sensitive cohort is moving despite the FOMC; Factor 1 leading indicator turning.
Any SFR REIT (INVH/AMH) 10-Q with >5% net dispositions when filings land mid-May — Factor 2 finally gets directional signal. Last filings were Feb; Q1 10-Qs due ~May 12-15.
First Warsh public statement on housing — whether he frames market as Fed-policy concern or tariff/fiscal concern sets tone for Sept FOMC.
April existing-home sales (May 11, NAR) — if SAAR <4.0M, confirms 2026 on track to undershoot 2024's 30-year low. Thesis-confirming on lockup; thesis-disconfirming on near-term unlock.
Oil sustained >$85/bbl through May — independent of Hormuz status, takes Sept cut off the table entirely.

days_stale=212; substituting FHFA HPI as price reference.
days_stale=120; Q1 2026 print due late April per Census schedule.
period_end_date is null for all REITs; only filing_date populated. Cannot compute QoQ home-count deltas. sec_reit_homes.csv may have it but not flowing into JSON.
MHO ASP shows 459, MTH ASP shows 373 — clearly truncated thousands. QoQ '-100%' is artifact, not real.
quality_flags=rate_bps_diff on every entry; differencing transform may inflate near-zero r values relative to true levels-correlation.
